stem: A car's value depreciates by 15% in the first year and then by 10% in the second year. If the car is worth $30,600 after two years, what was its original purchase price in dollars?
explanation: Let P be the original price. After 15% depreciation in year 1, the value is P - 0.15P = 0.85P. After 10% depreciation in year 2, the value is 0.85P - 0.10(0.85P) = 0.85P(1 - 0.10) = 0.85P × 0.90 = 0.765P. Setting this equal to the final value: 0.765P = 30,600. Solving for P: P = 30,600 ÷ 0.765 = 40,000. The original purchase price was $40,000.

distractorRationale.A: Reversed only the second year depreciation and stopped: 30,600 ÷ 0.90 = 34,000.
distractorRationale.B: Added the depreciation percentages back to the final value instead of dividing: 30,600 × 1.25 = 38,250.
distractorRationale.C: Correct: after 15% then 10% successive depreciation, value is 0.85 × 0.90 × P = 0.765P = 30,600, so P = 40,000.
distractorRationale.D: Combined depreciation as a single 25% decline by adding 15% + 10%: 30,600 ÷ 0.75 = 40,800.
